steps.title: Convert radicals to exponents
steps.content:
Rewrite each radical using a fractional exponent.
$$\sqrt[5]{84n}(\sqrt[6]{84n})^2=(84n)^{1/5}((84n)^{1/6})^2$$

steps.title: Combine the exponents
steps.content:
Use the power rule, then add exponents with the same base.
$$((84n)^{1/6})^2=(84n)^{1/3}$$
$$\frac{1}{5}+\frac{1}{3}=\frac{3}{15}+\frac{5}{15}=\frac{8}{15}$$

steps.title: Match the target exponent
steps.content:
The expression is equivalent to $(84n)^{8/15}$, and the target form is $(84n)^{30x}$. Set the exponents equal.
$$\frac{8}{15}=30x$$
$$x=\frac{8}{450}=\frac{4}{225}$$
The answer is 4/225.
